```{r}
  shared_transsignals[gene, "n_trans_rare"] <- length(setdiff(trans_rare, trans_common))
  shared_transsignals[gene, "n_trans_common"] <- length(setdiff(trans_common, trans_rare))
  shared_transsignals[gene, "genes_shared"] <- paste(intersect(trans_rare, trans_common), collapse = ";")
  shared_transsignals[gene, "genes_rare"] <- paste(setdiff(trans_rare, trans_common), collapse = ";")
  shared_transsignals[gene, "genes_common"] <- paste(setdiff(trans_common, trans_rare), collapse = ";")
}

# Bind number of gene within +- 1Mb
shared_transsignals <- shared_transsignals |>
  left_join(pqtls_assocs_joined_relaxed[,c("proximal_gene", "n_codinggenes_1mb")] |>
              dplyr::arrange(desc(n_codinggenes_1mb)) |> 
              dplyr::filter(duplicated(proximal_gene) == FALSE), by = "proximal_gene")

# Proportion of the total trans hits seen with variants in a gene which are detected using both common and rare instruments?
shared_transsignals$prop_shared = shared_transsignals$n_trans_shared/shared_transsignals$n_trans_total

# shared_transsignals |> dplyr::filter(n_trans_total != 0) |>
#   ggplot(aes(x = n_codinggenes_1mb, y = prop_shared)) +
#   geom_point()

```

### IV heterogenity

Heterogenity in SNP effects can be a result of variants acting within differing biological pathways to infuence an outcome (pleiotropy), and violates the third IV assumption (an instrument only influences an outcome via its effect on an exposure). Do rare and common, cis and trans IVs exhibit differing heterogenity in causal effect estimates? When rare and common instruments are combined into one MR analysis, is there a relationship between exposure effect and SNP heterogenity (definied as the deviation between the wald ratio for an IV and the IVW estimated from the metanalysis of all IVs)?...

```{r}
#| label: IV-heterogenity
#| fig.height: 20
#| fig.width: 5

# Read in results of mr_heterogenity() for common and rare variant IV sets
files_het <- list.files(res_dir, pattern = "heterogenity", full.names = T)
het <- do.call("rbind", lapply(files_het, function(x){data.table::fread(x)}))

het_set <- het |> 
  dplyr::filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted" & n_snps > 5) |>
  dplyr::mutate(IV_set = factor(IVsets[variant_set], levels = variant_IVsets))

het_set|> 
  ggplot(aes(x = variant_type, y = Q)) +
  facet_grid(outcome ~ IV_set) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = IV_set)) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) + 
  labs(x = "", y = "Q statistic", title = "Heterogenity in causal estimates (proteins with >5 IVs)")

# Restrict to the same proteins across instrument sets (sensitivity analysis)

# comp_hetcis <- het_set |> dplyr::filter(variant_type == "cis") |> with(pairwise.wilcox.test(Q, IV_set, p.adjust.method = "none"))
# comp_hetcis$p.value
# comp_hettrans <- het_set |> dplyr::filter(variant_type == "trans") |> with(pairwise.wilcox.test(Q, IV_set, p.adjust.method = "none"))
# comp_hettrans$p.value
```

## Causal effect estimates

### Comparison of causal effects

**Significant causal effects detected across instrument types**

```{r}
#| label: full-results
#| fig.width: 12
#| fig.height: 33
#| cache: TRUE

# P-value threshold for cis-MR (multiple testing correction for proteins instrumentable with at least 3 IVs in cis)
alpha_cis3 <- 0.05/max(avail_IVs_relaxed |> dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis") |> pull(n_prots_3IV)) # 5x10^-5

# Plot "manhattan" for protein causal effects on a given outcome, restricting to IV sets of at least 3 variants (masks can have 1)
plot_fullresults <- function(results_MR, outcome, nsnps = 3, cistrans = "cis"){
  df <- do.call("rbind", lapply(results_MR[[outcome]], function(x){
    dat <- x |> 
      dplyr::filter(method %in% c("Wald ratio", "Inverse variance weighted"),
                    cis_trans == cistrans,
                    (IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets) & nsnp >= nsnps) | !(IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets)),
                    IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets))
  }))
  # Format for plotting
  df <- df |>
    dplyr::arrange(pval) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      set = IVsets[IV_set],
      col = factor(ifelse(pval <= 0.01, set, NA), levels = IVsets),
      label = ifelse(pval <= alpha, exposure, NA))
    #dplyr::mutate(label = ifelse(duplicated(label), NA, label))
  
  df |> dplyr::arrange(desc(pval)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = exposure, y = -log10(pval))) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(alpha_cis3), col = "grey50", lty = 2) +
    geom_point(aes(colour = col), size = 2.5) +
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = label)) +
    scale_colour_manual(name = "", values = cols_IVsets, na.value = "grey90") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          axis.text.x = element_blank(),
          axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
          axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
          panel.grid = element_blank(),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 12)) +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow = 1)) +
    labs(x = "Protein exposure", y = "IVW/WR -log10(P)", title = outcome)
}

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = 
  lapply(X = names(res_MR_relaxed), FUN = plot_fullresults, results_MR = res_MR_relaxed, nsnps = 3, cistrans = "cis"), nrow = 11)


```

How many new highly significant causal associations are detected with rare variants or gene based masks that a. cannot be instrumented in cis with common variants or b. show no causal effect when using common-IVs (suggesting rare variants perturb the gene in a more relevant fashion)?:

```{r}
#| label: causal-effect-overlap
#| cache: TRUE

# Pull significant MR results (at p<5e-05 for comparison of cis IV sets)
# >=3 IVs for variant-based instruments, 1 sufficient for masks (exclude masks for now?)

sig_MR_relaxed <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, function(x){
  do.call("rbind", lapply(x, function(y){
    dat <- y |> 
      dplyr::filter(method %in% c("Wald ratio", "Inverse variance weighted"),
                    cis_trans == "cis",
                    (IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets) & nsnp >= 3) | !(IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets)),
                    IV_set %in% names(paper_IVsets),
                    pval <= alpha_cis3)})) |> # multiple testing correction
    dplyr::mutate(IVset = IVsets[IV_set])})

# Upset plot of overlap in support for exposure --> outcome association
upset_sig_cis <- lapply(sig_MR_relaxed, function(x){
  list("mask:pLOF|miss" = x |> dplyr::filter(IVset %in% c("mask:pLOF", "mask:miss", "mask:pLOF|miss")) |>  dplyr::pull(pair) |> unique(),
       "IV:rare" = x |> dplyr::filter(IVset %in% c("exome:ultrarare","exome:rare")) |>  dplyr::pull(pair) |> unique(),
       "IV:common" = x |> dplyr::filter(IVset %in% c("exome:common", "genome:common")) |>  dplyr::pull(pair) |> unique())})
upset_sig_cis <- purrr::map(names(upset_sig_cis[[1]]), ~ purrr::flatten_chr(purrr::map(upset_sig_cis, .x)))
names(upset_sig_cis) <- c("mask:pLOF|miss","variant:rare","variant:common")

# Pull significant MR results (at p<0.01 for comparison of cis IV sets)
# >=3 IVs for variant-based instruments, 1 sufficient for masks (exclude masks for now?)

sig_MR_relaxed_0.01 <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, function(x){
  do.call("rbind", lapply(x, function(y){
    dat <- y |> 
      dplyr::filter(method %in% c("Wald ratio", "Inverse variance weighted"),
                    cis_trans == "cis",
                    (IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets) & nsnp >= 3) | !(IV_set %in% names(variant_IVsets)),
                    IV_set %in% names(paper_IVsets),
                    pval <= 0.01)})) |> # multiple testing correction
    dplyr::mutate(IVset = IVsets[IV_set])})

# Upset plot of overlap in support for exposure --> outcome association
upset_sig_cis_0.01 <- lapply(sig_MR_relaxed_0.01, function(x){
  list("mask:pLOF|miss" = x |> dplyr::filter(IVset %in% c("mask:pLOF", "mask:miss", "mask:pLOF|miss")) |>  dplyr::pull(pair) |> unique(),
       "IV:rare" = x |> dplyr::filter(IVset %in% c("exome:ultrarare","exome:rare")) |>  dplyr::pull(pair) |> unique(),
       "IV:common" = x |> dplyr::filter(IVset %in% c("exome:common", "genome:common")) |>  dplyr::pull(pair) |> unique())})
upset_sig_cis_0.01 <- purrr::map(names(upset_sig_cis_0.01[[1]]), ~ purrr::flatten_chr(purrr::map(upset_sig_cis_0.01, .x)))
names(upset_sig_cis_0.01) <- c("mask:pLOF|miss","variant:rare","variant:common")

## Exposure:outcome pairs with sufficient cis-IVs to test
common_pairs <- num_IVs_relaxed |> dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis", grepl("common", IV_set), value >= 3) |> pull(name) |> unique()
rare_pairs <- num_IVs_relaxed |> dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis", grepl("rare_pruned", IV_set), value >= 3) |> pull(name) |> unique()

## Overlap in sig exposure-outcome pairs (P<5e-5)
g1 <- ggvenn::ggvenn(upset_sig_cis[c(2,3)], fill_color = c("steelblue1","orange"), set_name_size = 5) + 
  labs(title = "Exposure:outcome pair \nP(IVW)<5e-5") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
### Of rare unique:
### Lack of common IVs
table(g1$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE & `variant:common` == FALSE) |> pull(`_key`) %in% common_pairs)
### Significant at lower threshold?
table(g1$data |>
  dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE & `variant:common` == FALSE) |>
  dplyr::filter(`_key` %in% common_pairs) |>
  pull(`_key`) %in% upset_sig_cis_0.01$`variant:common`) # 1 yes, 10 no

### Of common unique:
### Lack of rare IVs
table(g1$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == FALSE & `variant:common` == TRUE) |> pull(`_key`) %in% rare_pairs)
### Significant at lower threshold?
table(g1$data |>
  dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == FALSE & `variant:common` == TRUE) |>
  dplyr::filter(`_key` %in% rare_pairs) |>
  pull(`_key`) %in% upset_sig_cis_0.01$`variant:rare`) # 18 yes, 81 no
### Of shared:
### Concordant vs discordant direction of effect
shared_5e5 <- g1$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE & `variant:common` == TRUE) |> pull(`_key`)
concordant_5e5 <- do.call("rbind",lapply(sig_MR_relaxed, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(pair %in% shared_5e5)})) |>
  dplyr::filter(nsnp >= 3) |>
  group_by(pair) |>
  summarise(length(unique(sign(b))))
## Concordant sign?
table(concordant_5e5[,2]==1)

## Overlap in sig exposure-outcome pairs (P<5e-5)
g1_all <- ggvenn::ggvenn(upset_sig_cis, fill_color = c("mediumorchid","steelblue1","orange"), set_name_size = 5) + 
  labs(title = "Exposure:outcome pair \nP(IVW)<5e-5") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

## Overlap in sig exposure-outcome pairs (P<0.01)
g2 <- ggvenn::ggvenn(upset_sig_cis_0.01[c(2,3)], fill_color = c("steelblue1","orange"), set_name_size = 5) +
  labs(title = "Exposure:outcome pair \nP(IVW)<0.01") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
### Of rare unique:
### Lack of common IVs
table(g2$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE & `variant:common` == FALSE) |> pull(`_key`) %in% common_pairs)
### Which pairs are these?:
# g2$data |>
#   dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE & `variant:common` == FALSE) |>
#   dplyr::mutate(commonIVs_exist = `_key` %in% common_pairs)
### Of common unique:
### Lack of rare IVs
table(g2$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == FALSE & `variant:common` == TRUE) |> pull(`_key`) %in% rare_pairs)
### Which pairs are these?:
# g2$data |>
#   dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == FALSE & `variant:common` == TRUE) |>
#   dplyr::mutate(rareIVs_exist = `_key` %in% rare_pairs)
### Of shared:
### Concordant vs discordant direction of effect
shared_0.01 <- g2$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE & `variant:common` == TRUE) |> pull(`_key`)
concordant_0.01 <- do.call("rbind",lapply(sig_MR_relaxed_0.01, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(pair %in% shared_0.01)})) |>
  dplyr::filter(nsnp >= 3) |>
  group_by(pair) |>
  summarise(length(unique(sign(b))))
## Concordant sign?
table(concordant_0.01[,2]==1)

# 38 causal protein --> outcome relationships when instrumenting using rare cis variants (p<5x10^-5)
# 31 undetected when using common cis-IVs at this threshold
## Of these 31:
### 20 lacked sufficient common-IVs and were not tested
### 1 are significant using common-IVs at a relaxed threshold 0.01>p>5x10^-5
### 10 were tested but using common-IVs but no causal associaton was detected

# 131 causal protein --> outcome relationships when instrumenteing using common cis variants
# 124 undetected when using rare cis-IVs at this threshold
### 25 lacked sufficient rare-IVs and were not teted
### 18 are significant using rare-IVs at a relaxed threshold 0.01>p>5x10^-5
### 81 were tested but using rare-IVs but no causal associaton was detected

```

```{r}
#| label: venns
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 7

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = list(g1,g2), nrow = 1)
gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = list(g1_all), nrow = 1)
```

**Example results**

Plot effect estimates across IV sets for a given exposure-outcome pair

```{r}
#| label: plot-mr-results

plot_results <- function(exposure, outcome){
  
  pair <- paste(exposure, outcome, sep = "_")
  # Individual SNP results
  dat_snps <- do.call("rbind",lapply(harmonised_snps_relaxed[[outcome]][[pair]][names(paper_IVsets)], function(x){
    x |> dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis", mr_keep == TRUE) |>
      dplyr::select(SNP, beta.exposure, beta.outcome, pval.exposure, pval.outcome, se.exposure, se.outcome)})) |>
    tibble::rownames_to_column("IV_set") |>
    dplyr::mutate(IV_set = factor(IVsets[sub("\\..*","",IV_set)], levels = IVsets)) |>
    dplyr::filter(IV_set %in% paper_IVsets) |>
    dplyr::arrange(desc(IV_set))
  
  # MR estimates
  dat_res <- res_MR_relaxed[[outcome]][[pair]] |> 
    dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis", method %in% c("Wald ratio", "Inverse variance weighted"), IV_set %in% names(paper_IVsets)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(IV_set = factor(IVsets[IV_set], levels = IVsets))
  
  ## IVW plot
  p_slope <- dat_snps |> dplyr::filter(IV_set %in% variant_IVsets) |> 
    ggplot(aes(x = beta.exposure, y = beta.outcome, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey70") +
    geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = beta.exposure - 1.96*se.exposure, xmax = beta.exposure + 1.96*se.exposure), height = 0, alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = beta.outcome - 1.96*se.outcome, ymax = beta.outcome + 1.96*se.outcome), height = 0, alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_abline(data = dat_res |> dplyr::filter(nsnp > 1), aes(slope = b, intercept = 0, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_point(size = 2) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow = 2)) +
    labs(x = expression(beta["exposure"]), y = expression(beta["outcome"]), title = paste("Exposure:", exposure, "Outcome:", outcome))
    
  ## Forest plot
  p_forest_vars <- dat_res |> dplyr::filter(IV_set %in% variant_IVsets) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = b, y = IV_set, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point(aes(size = nsnp)) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = b-1.96*se, xmax = b+1.96*se, group = cis_trans), height = 0.4) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    scale_size_area() +
    theme_minimal() + guides(colour = "none") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
          legend.position = "right") +
    labs(x = "IVW", y = "", size = "n(IVs)")
  
  p_forest_mask <- dat_res |> dplyr::filter(!(IV_set %in% variant_IVsets)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = b, y = IV_set, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = b-1.96*se, xmax = b+1.96*se, group = cis_trans), height = 0.4) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    scale_size_area() +
    theme_minimal() + guides(colour = "none") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), 
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7),
          legend.position = "right") +
    labs(x = "WR", y = "", size = "n(IVs)")
  
  cowplot::ggdraw() + cowplot::draw_plot(p_slope,0,0,0.5,1) + 
    cowplot::draw_plot(p_forest_vars,0.5,0.2,0.5,0.4) +
    cowplot::draw_plot(p_forest_mask,0.5,0.6,0.4,0.2)
}
```

```{r}
#| label: plot-mr-results-explore
#| include: FALSE

## Plot, common variant associations lacking rare evidence
common_only <- g2$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == FALSE, `variant:common` == TRUE) |> dplyr::pull(`_key`)
common_only <- common_only[common_only %in% upset_sig_cis$`variant:common`] # Significant w FDR correction

apply(as.data.frame(do.call("rbind",(strsplit(common_only, "_")))), MARGIN = 1,
                    FUN = function(x){plot_results(exposure = x["V1"], outcome = x["V2"])})

# CLMP-SBP
# HDDC2-RBCcount (four rare variants in gene)
# MPO-CorpVol

## Plot, rare variant associations lacking common evidence
rare_only <- g2$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE, `variant:common` == FALSE) |> dplyr::pull(`_key`)
rare_only <- rare_only[rare_only %in% upset_sig_cis$`variant:rare`] # Significant w FDR correction

apply(as.data.frame(do.call("rbind",(strsplit(rare_only, "_")))), MARGIN = 1,
                    FUN = function(x){plot_results(exposure = x["V1"], outcome = x["V2"])})

# Shared
shared <- g1$data |> dplyr::filter(`variant:rare` == TRUE, `variant:common` == TRUE) |> dplyr::pull(`_key`)

apply(as.data.frame(do.call("rbind",(strsplit(shared, "_")))), MARGIN = 1,
                    FUN = function(x){plot_results(exposure = x["V1"], outcome = x["V2"])})
```


```{r}
#| label: plot-mr-results-examples
#| fig.width: 14
#| fig.height: 8.5

## Example of concordant results from instrument sets (PRSS53 in BMI)
#plot_results(exposure = "PRSS53", outcome = "BMI")
ex1 <- plot_results(exposure = "AMPD3", outcome = "HbA1c")
ex2 <- plot_results(exposure = "PCSK9", outcome = "LDL") ## Lack of common IVs

## Example of rare eviedence only 
#plot_results(exposure = "GMPR", outcome = "RBCcount") ## Lack of common IVs
ex3 <- plot_results(exposure = "SMPD1", outcome = "SBP")
#ex3 <- plot_results(exposure = "VNN1", outcome = "VitD")
## Example of common evidence only
#ex4 <- plot_results(exposure = "PRSS53", outcome = "LDL")
#ex4 <- plot_results(exposure = "CDSN", outcome = "PlatVol")
ex4 <- plot_results(exposure = "HDDC2", outcome = "RBCcount")

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = list(ex1,ex2,ex3,ex4), nrow = 2)
```

**Forest plots of wald ratios**

```{r}
#| label: case-study-wrs

# Pull IVs used for MR (across variant sets), derive wald ratios and annotate exome variants with amino acid change
annotate_ivs <- function(outcome, exposure, snpset){
  # Pull cis variants
  ivs_exposure <- lapply(snpset[[outcome]][[paste(exposure,outcome,sep = "_")]], function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis")})
  ivs_exposure <- ivs_exposure[unlist(lapply(ivs_exposure, nrow)) > 0]
  
  # Derive wald ratios for all cis-IVs, all frequencies
  keep_cols <- c("SNP","variant","chr.exposure","pos.exposure","beta.exposure","beta.outcome",
                 "se.exposure","se.outcome","pval.exposure","pval.outcome",
                 "eaf.exposure","cis_trans","gene.outcome", "ANNOTATON")
  
  ## Preformat data
  ivs_exposure_all <- do.call("bind_rows", mapply(FUN = function(x,y){
    keep_cols_df <- intersect(keep_cols, colnames(x))
    x <- x[,keep_cols_df]
    x$IV_set <- y
    return(x)}, x = ivs_exposure[names(variant_IVsets)], y = variant_IVsets, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(IV_set = factor(IV_set, levels = variant_IVsets[c(1,2,4,3)]),
                  chr.exposure = ifelse(grepl("rs", SNP), sub(":.*","",variant), chr.exposure),
                  pos.exposure = ifelse(grepl("rs", SNP), sub(".*:(.*)_.*_.*","\\1",variant), pos.exposure),
                  SNP = toupper(ifelse(grepl("rs", SNP), variant, SNP))) |>
    dplyr::arrange(pos.exposure, IV_set) |>
    dplyr::filter(duplicated(SNP) == FALSE, !is.na(SNP))
  
  ## WRs
  wrs_all <- t(apply(ivs_exposure_all, MARGIN = 1, function(x){
    TwoSampleMR::mr_wald_ratio(b_exp = as.numeric(x["beta.exposure"]),
                               b_out = as.numeric(x["beta.outcome"]), 
                               se_exp = as.numeric(x["se.exposure"]),
                               se_out = as.numeric(x["se.outcome"])) |> unlist()
  }))
    
  colnames(wrs_all) <- paste0("wald_ratio_", colnames(wrs_all))
  wrs_all <- cbind(ivs_exposure_all, wrs_all)
  
  # Annotate exome variants
  deets_snps <- wes_deets |> dplyr::mutate(SNP_join = gsub("-","_",sub("-",":",Variant)))

  wrs_all <- left_join(wrs_all, 
                       deets_snps[,c("SNP_join","cDNA change","Amino acid change","Exon rank","ANNOTATION")],
                       by = c("SNP" = "SNP_join")) |>
    dplyr::mutate(exposure = exposure, outcome = outcome)
  
  return(wrs_all)
}

# Plot
# ivs = harmonised SNP data with appendend wald ratios and variant details
# orientate = TRUE (orientate to expression increasing allele)
plot_forest_snps <- function(iv_effects, orientate){
  iv_effects$SNP_name = ifelse(!is.na(iv_effects$`Amino acid change`) & grepl("exome",as.character(iv_effects$IV_set)),
                               paste0("chr",sub("_.*","",iv_effects$SNP), " (", iv_effects$`Amino acid change`, " ", iv_effects$gene.outcome, ")"),
                               paste0("chr",sub("_.*","",iv_effects$SNP)))
    
   if(orientate == TRUE){
     flip_sign <- ifelse(iv_effects$beta.exposure < 0, TRUE, FALSE)
     iv_effects$beta.exposure[flip_sign] <- iv_effects$beta.exposure[flip_sign]*-1
     iv_effects$beta.outcome[flip_sign] <- iv_effects$beta.outcome[flip_sign]*-1
   }
  
  ntests <- nrow(iv_effects)
  exposure <- unique(iv_effects$exposure)
  outcome <- unique(iv_effects$outcome)
  
  f1 <- iv_effects |>
    dplyr::mutate(col_points = ifelse(pval.exposure < 1, as.character(IV_set), NA)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = SNP_name, y = beta.exposure, colour = col_points)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = beta.exposure - 1.96*se.exposure,
                      ymax = beta.exposure + 1.96*se.exposure), width = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets, na.value = "grey90") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "", y = "Bx")
  
  f2 <- iv_effects |>
    dplyr::mutate(col_points = ifelse(pval.outcome < 1, as.character(IV_set), NA)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = SNP_name, y = beta.outcome, colour = col_points)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = beta.outcome - 1.96*se.outcome,
                      ymax = beta.outcome + 1.96*se.outcome), width = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets, na.value = "grey90") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "", y = "By")
  
  f3 <- iv_effects |>
    dplyr::mutate(col_points = ifelse(wald_ratio_pval < 1, as.character(IV_set), NA)) |>
    ggplot(aes(x = SNP_name, y = wald_ratio_b, colour = col_points)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = wald_ratio_b - 1.96*wald_ratio_se,
                      ymax = wald_ratio_b + 1.96*wald_ratio_se), width = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets, na.value = "grey90") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 75, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "", y = "By/Bx")
  
  gridExtra::grid.arrange(f1, f2, f3, heights = c(0.2, 0.2, 0.48), top = paste0(exposure, " IVs ", exposure, ":", outcome))
}
```

```{r}
#| label: AMPD3
#| fig.width: 4
#| fig.height: 8

plot_forest_snps(iv_effects = annotate_ivs(outcome = "HbA1c", exposure = "AMPD3", snpset = harmonised_snps_relaxed), orientate = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: PCSK9
#| fig.width: 2
#| fig.height: 8

plot_forest_snps(iv_effects = annotate_ivs(outcome = "LDL", exposure = "PCSK9", snpset = harmonised_snps_relaxed), orientate = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: SMPD1
#| fig.width: 6
#| fig.height: 8

plot_forest_snps(iv_effects = annotate_ivs(outcome = "SBP", exposure = "SMPD1", snpset = harmonised_snps_relaxed), orientate = TRUE)
```

```{r}
#| label: HDDC2
#| fig.width: 4
#| fig.height: 8

plot_forest_snps(iv_effects = annotate_ivs(outcome = "RBCcount", exposure = "HDDC2", snpset = harmonised_snps_relaxed), orientate = TRUE)
```

**Comparison of IVW effect estimates across instrument sets**

Given the relationships shown above, particularly that reducing the stringency of significance thresholding for rare IVs does not reduce instrument specificity (pleiotropy profiles remain consistant), I have compared IVW effect estimates for protein --> outcome relationships instrumented using at least three common (at P<5e-8) and at least three rare (at P<1e-4) cis or trans instruments.

```{r}
#| label: compare estimates
#| cache: TRUE
#| message: FALSE

# Functions
# Generate a table of IVW estimates for a pair of instrument sets
compare_estimates <- function(results_MR, IVset1, IVset2, n_snps=3, cis_trans="all"){
  # Pull out IVW estimates and SE for instrument sets
  if(cis_trans == "all"){
    df <- lapply(results_MR, function(x){
      x |> dplyr::filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted", 
                         cis_trans == "cis_trans",
                         IV_set %in% c(IVset1,IVset2))
    })
  }
  if(cis_trans == "cis"){
    df <- lapply(results_MR, function(x){
      x |> dplyr::filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted", 
                         cis_trans == "cis",
                         IV_set %in% c(IVset1,IVset2))
    })
  }
  if(cis_trans == "trans"){
    df <- lapply(results_MR, function(x){
      x |> dplyr::filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted", 
                         cis_trans == "trans",
                         IV_set %in% c(IVset1,IVset2))
    })
  }
  df_out_b <- do.call("rbind",df) |> 
    dplyr::filter(nsnp >= n_snps) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "IV_set", values_from = c("b"), id_cols = "exposure") |>
    na.exclude() |>
    dplyr::relocate(exposure,IVset1,IVset2)
  
  df_out_se <- do.call("rbind",df) |> 
    dplyr::filter(nsnp >= n_snps) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "IV_set", values_from = "se", id_cols = "exposure") |>
    na.exclude() |>
    dplyr::relocate(exposure,IVset1,IVset2)
  
  df_out_p <- do.call("rbind",df) |> 
    dplyr::filter(nsnp >= n_snps) |>
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "IV_set", values_from = "pval", id_cols = "exposure") |>
    na.exclude() |>
    dplyr::relocate(exposure,IVset1,IVset2)
  colnames(df_out_p)[2:3] <- paste0(colnames(df_out_p)[2:3],".pval")
  
  ## Caluclate effect heterogeneity
  if (!(identical(df_out_b$exposure, df_out_se$exposure) &&
        identical(colnames(df_out_b), colnames(df_out_se)))) {
    message("Error - mismatch of beta and SE dataframes")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  beta_vecs <- lapply(split(df_out_b[,c(2,3)], 1:nrow(df_out_b)), as.numeric)
  se_vecs <- lapply(split(df_out_se[,c(2,3)], 1:nrow(df_out_se)), as.numeric)
  
  het_res <- do.call("rbind", mapply(effect_heterogeneity, beta_vec = beta_vecs, se_vec = se_vecs, SIMPLIFY = F)) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    dplyr::mutate(exposure = df_out_b$exposure, .before = "Q") |>
    dplyr::mutate(Qpval_bon = p.adjust(Qpval, method = "bonferroni"),
                  Qpval_fdr = p.adjust(Qpval, method = "fdr"))
  
  df_out <- dplyr::left_join(df_out_b, df_out_se, by = "exposure", suffix = c(".beta",".se")) |>
    dplyr::left_join(df_out_p, by = "exposure") |>
    dplyr::left_join(het_res, by = "exposure")
  
  return(df_out)
}

# Generate a table of cis vs trans IVW estimates for given instrument set
compare_cistrans <- function(results_MR, IVset, n_snps=3){
  # Pull out IVW estimates and SE for instrument sets
  df <- lapply(results_MR, function(x){
    x |> 
      dplyr::filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted",
                    IV_set %in% IVset,
                    nsnp >= n_snps,
                    cis_trans %in% c("cis","trans")) |>
      dplyr::mutate(IV_set = paste(IV_set, cis_trans, sep = "_")) |>
      dplyr::arrange(cis_trans)})
  df <- df[lapply(df, nrow) == 2]
  
  df_out_b <- do.call("rbind",df) |> 
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "IV_set", values_from = "b", id_cols = "exposure") |>
    na.exclude()
  
  df_out_se <- do.call("rbind",df) |> 
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "IV_set", values_from = "se", id_cols = "exposure") |>
    na.exclude()
  
  df_out_p <- do.call("rbind",df) |> 
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = "IV_set", values_from = "pval", id_cols = "exposure") |>
    na.exclude()
  colnames(df_out_p)[2:3] <- paste0(colnames(df_out_p)[2:3],".pval")
  
  ## Caluclate effect heterogeneity
  if (!(identical(df_out_b$exposure, df_out_se$exposure) &&
        identical(colnames(df_out_b), colnames(df_out_se)))) {
    message("Error - mismatch of beta and SE dataframes")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  beta_vecs <- lapply(split(df_out_b[,c(2,3)], 1:nrow(df_out_b)), as.numeric)
  se_vecs <- lapply(split(df_out_se[,c(2,3)], 1:nrow(df_out_se)), as.numeric)
  
  het_res <- do.call("rbind", mapply(effect_heterogeneity, beta_vec = beta_vecs, se_vec = se_vecs, SIMPLIFY = F)) |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    dplyr::mutate(exposure = df_out_b$exposure, .before = "Q") |>
    dplyr::mutate(Qpval_bon = p.adjust(Qpval, method = "bonferroni"),
                  Qpval_fdr = p.adjust(Qpval, method = "fdr"))
  
  df_out <- dplyr::left_join(df_out_b, df_out_se, by = "exposure", suffix = c(".beta",".se")) |>
    dplyr::left_join(df_out_p, by = "exposure") |>
    dplyr::left_join(het_res, by = "exposure")
  
  return(df_out)
}

# Proportion of concordant/discordant MR estimates based on heterogenity estimates and direction of effect
prop_concordant <- function(estimates, pval_thresh = 0.05){
  
  estimate_cols <- grep("\\.beta",colnames(estimates), value = T)
  pval_cols <- grep("\\.pval",colnames(estimates), value = T)
  sets <- sub(".beta", "", estimate_cols) # IV sets being compared
  
  df_counts <- data.frame(n_instrumented_genes = nrow(estimates),
                       n_nohet = sum(estimates$Qpval > pval_thresh),
                       n_het_concordant = sum(estimates$Qpval <= pval_thresh & sign(estimates[estimate_cols[1]]) == sign(estimates[estimate_cols[2]])),
                       n_het_discordant = sum(estimates$Qpval <= pval_thresh & sign(estimates[estimate_cols[1]]) != sign(estimates[estimate_cols[2]])))
  
  df_props <- df_counts[,2:4]/df_counts[,1]
  colnames(df_props) <- sub("n_","prop_",colnames(df_props))
  
  df_out <- cbind(df_counts, df_props)
  
  # Discordant results
  estimates_het <- estimates |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= pval_thresh)
  
  df_counts_het <- data.frame(n_het_genes = nrow(estimates_het),
                              sig1 = sum(estimates_het[pval_cols[1]] < estimates_het[pval_cols[2]] & estimates_het[pval_cols[2]] > pval_thresh),
                              sig2 = sum(estimates_het[pval_cols[2]] < estimates_het[pval_cols[1]] & estimates_het[pval_cols[1]] > pval_thresh),
                              n_both_sig_concordant = sum(estimates_het[pval_cols[1]] < pval_thresh & estimates_het[pval_cols[2]] < pval_thresh & 
                                                    sign(estimates_het[estimate_cols[1]]) == sign(estimates_het[estimate_cols[2]])),
                              n_both_sig_discordant = sum(estimates_het[pval_cols[1]] < pval_thresh & estimates_het[pval_cols[2]] < pval_thresh & 
                                                    sign(estimates_het[estimate_cols[1]]) != sign(estimates_het[estimate_cols[2]])))
  
  colnames(df_counts_het)[2] <- paste0("n_",sets[1],"_sig")
  colnames(df_counts_het)[3] <- paste0("n_",sets[2],"_sig")
  
  df_het_props <- df_counts_het[,2:5]/df_counts_het[,1]
  colnames(df_het_props) <- sub("n_","prop_",colnames(df_het_props))
  
  df_out_het <- cbind(df_counts_het, df_het_props)
  
  return(list(all = df_out, sigQ = df_out_het))
}

plot_concordance <- function(concordance, title){
  
  plot_dat_1 <- concordance$all |> 
    tibble::rownames_to_column("outcome") |> 
    dplyr::mutate(n_het = n_instrumented_genes - n_nohet) |>
    dplyr::select(c(1,3,9)) |> tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(2:3))
  
  p1 <- plot_dat_1 |> ggplot(aes(x = outcome, y = value, fill = name)) +
    geom_col(position = "fill") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    labs(x = "Outcome", 
         y = " Proportion of MR results \n(protein --> outcome)") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "",
      values = c("tomato","grey90"), 
      labels = c("Qpval<thresh","Qpval>thresh")) +
    geom_text(dat = plot_dat_1 |> dplyr::filter(name == "n_het"), aes(x = outcome, y = 1.05, label = value), colour = "tomato") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.key.size = unit(0.6, "cm"),
          legend.position = "bottom") +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))
  
  plot_dat_2 <- concordance$Qsig |> 
    tibble::rownames_to_column("outcome") |> 
    dplyr::select(c(1,3:6)) |> tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = c(2:5))
  
  sets = unique(plot_dat_2$name)[1:2]
  sets = sub("_sig$","",sub("^n_","",sets))
  
  if(all(grepl("cis|trans", sets))){
    fill_names = c(paste(IVsets[sub("_(cis|trans)$","",sets)],c("(cis)","(trans)")), "Sig. conc.", "Sig. dis.")
    names(fill_names) <- unique(plot_dat_2$name)
    fill_col = c("grey", "grey30","tomato","steelblue1")
  }else{
    fill_names = c(IVsets[sets], "Sig. conc.", "Sig. dis.")
    names(fill_names) <- unique(plot_dat_2$name)
    fill_col = c(as.character(cols_IVsets[fill_names[1:2]]), "grey", "grey30")
    names(fill_col) <- names(fill_names)
  }
  
  p2 <- plot_dat_2 |> ggplot(aes(x = outcome, y = value, fill = name)) +
    geom_col(position = "fill") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    labs(x = "Outcome", 
         y = " Proportion of heterogenous MR results \n(protein --> outcome)") +
    scale_fill_manual(name = "",
      values = fill_col,
      labels = fill_names) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
          legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.key.size = unit(0.6, "cm")) +
    guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE))
  
  gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1,p2,nrow = 1, top = title)
  
}

plot_estimates <- function(estimates, name, pval_thresh = 0.05){
  
  estimates <- as.data.frame(estimates)
  col_names <- names(estimates)
  
  if(any(grepl("cis|trans",col_names))){
    lab_x <- "cis-IVs"
    lab_y <- "trans-IVs"
  } else {
    lab_x <- IVsets[sub("\\.beta","",col_names[2])]
    lab_y <- IVsets[sub("\\.beta","",col_names[3])]
  }
  
  CI_x <- data.frame(LCI = estimates[,2] - 1.96*estimates[,4], UCI = estimates[,2] + 1.96*estimates[,4])
  CI_y <- data.frame(LCI = estimates[,3] - 1.96*estimates[,5], UCI = estimates[,3] + 1.96*estimates[,5])
  
  mod_lm <- lm(estimates[,3] ~ estimates[,2])
  slope <- round(coefficients(mod_lm)[2],2)
  
  flag_point <- estimates$Qpval <= pval_thresh
  
  range_min <- min(min(estimates[,2]), min(estimates[,3])) #min(na.exclude(errorbar_xmin, errorbar_ymin)))
  range_max <- max(max(estimates[,2]), max(estimates[,3])) #min(na.exclude(errorbar_xmax, errorbar_ymax)))
  
  estimates_plot <- estimates |>
    dplyr::mutate(exposure = factor(exposure, levels = estimates$exposure[order(estimates$Qpval, decreasing = TRUE)]),
                  errorbar_xmin = ifelse(flag_point, CI_x$LCI, NA),
                  errorbar_xmax = ifelse(flag_point, CI_x$UCI, NA),
                  errorbar_ymin = ifelse(flag_point, CI_y$LCI, NA),
                  errorbar_ymax = ifelse(flag_point, CI_y$UCI, NA)) |>
    dplyr::arrange(exposure)
  
  estimates_plot |>
    ggplot(aes_string(x = col_names[2], y = col_names[3])) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, colour = "steelblue", lty = 2, lwd = 0.5) +
    geom_point(aes(colour = estimates_plot$Qpval <= pval_thresh), size = 2, shape = 16) +  
    geom_errorbar(xmin = estimates_plot$errorbar_xmin,
                  xmax = estimates_plot$errorbar_xmax, colour = "tomato") +
    geom_errorbar(ymin = estimates_plot$errorbar_ymin,
                  ymax = estimates_plot$errorbar_ymax, colour = "tomato") +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "tomato", lwd = 0.5) +
    annotate(geom = "text", x = range_min/2, y = range_max, label = paste0("Slope=",slope), size = 3, colour = "tomato") +
    scale_colour_manual(values = c("black","tomato")) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(range_min, range_max)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(range_min, range_max)) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x=lab_x, y=lab_y, title = name)
}

# Upset plot comparing signifiant causal estimates across variant sets
# Restrtcited to those proteins that could be instrumented with >= 3 variants across the comparison sets

plot_upset <- function(IVsets_list, cis_trans = "all", pval_thresh = 0.05){
  
  if(cis_trans == "all"){inlist = summary_sig}
  if(cis_trans == "cis"){inlist = summary_sig_cis}
  if(cis_trans == "trans"){inlist = summary_sig_trans}
  
  set_sig <- lapply(inlist, function(x){
    dat <- x$min_p[,IVsets_list]
    # Extract only those tests that could be conducted in all IV sets
    tests <- rownames(dat) # Exposure_outcome
    shared_tests <- tests[apply(dat, MARGIN = 1, function(x){sum(!(is.na(x)))}) == 2]
    dat <- dat[shared_tests,]
    dat_sig <- dat <= pval_thresh
    
    tests <- rownames(dat_sig)
    list_sig <- apply(dat_sig, MARGIN = 2, FUN = function(x){tests[x]}, simplify = F) # Names of significant exposures
    names(list_sig) <- IVsets[names(list_sig)]
    list_sig <- list_sig[lapply(list_sig, length) > 0] # Remove empty exposure sets
    return(list_sig)
  })
  
  # Upset plots
  plots <- mapply(FUN = function(list_sig, outcome){
    if(length(list_sig) == 1){return(NULL)}
    upset(fromList(list_sig), order.by = "freq", text.scale = 1.5, mainbar.y.label = paste0("Protein:",outcome,"\n (IVW p<", pval, ")"))
  }, list_sig = set_sig, outcome = names(set_sig), SIMPLIFY = F)
  
  plots <- plots[!sapply(plots, is.null)]
  
  # for (v in names(plots)) {
  #   print(plots[[v]])
  #   #grid.text(v, x = 0.65, y=0.97, gp = gpar(fontsize = 20))
  #   grid.edit('arrange', name = v)
  #   vp <- grid.grab()
  #   plots[[v]] <- vp
  # }
  return(plots)
}
```

1a) Common vs rare variant cis-IVs (ExWAS)\
1b) Common vs rare variant trans-IVs (ExWAS)

2a) Common vs ultra-rare variant cis-IVs (ExWAS)\
2b) Common vs ultra-rare variant trans-IVs (ExWAS)

```{r}
#| label: fig-compIVs-commonvrare
#| cache: TRUE
#| message: FALSE
#| fig.width: 14
#| fig.height: 6

comp_IVWs_1a <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_common",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "cis")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_1a_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_1a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_1a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_1a, name = names(comp_IVWs_1a), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from WES common and rare cis-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_1b <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_common",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "trans")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_1b_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_1b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_1b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_1b, name = names(comp_IVWs_1b), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from WES common and rare trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_2a <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_common",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "cis")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_2a_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_2a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_2a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_2a, name = names(comp_IVWs_2a), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from WES common and ultra-rare cis-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_2b <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_common",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "trans")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_2b_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_2b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_2b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_2b, name = names(comp_IVWs_2b), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from WES common and ultra-rare trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-compIVs-commonvrare-concordance
#| fig.width: 7
#| fig.height: 5

plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_1a_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES common vs rare cis-IVs")
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_1b_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES common vs rare trans-IVs") 

plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_2a_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES common vs ultrarare cis-IVs")
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_2b_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES common vs ultrarare trans-IVs") 

# Mean concordance/discordance (across outcomes)
colMeans(comp_IVWs_1a_conc$all) # cis-common v rare
colMeans(comp_IVWs_2a_conc$all) # cis-common v ultrarare
```

3a) Common (GWAS) vs rare variant cis-IVs (ExWAS)\
3b) Common (GWAS) vs rare variant trans-IVs (ExWAS)

4a) Common (GWAS) vs ultra-rare variant cis-IVs (ExWAS)\
4b) Common (GWAS) vs ultra-rare variant trans-IVs (ExWAS)

```{r}
#| label: fig-compIVs-commonGWASvrare
#| cache: TRUE
#| message: FALSE
#| fig.width: 14
#| fig.height: 6

comp_IVWs_3a <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "cis")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_3a_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_3a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_3a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_3a, name = names(comp_IVWs_3a), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from GWAS common and WES rare cis-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_3b <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "trans")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_3b_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_3b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_3b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_3b, name = names(comp_IVWs_3b), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from GWAS  common and WES rare trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_4a <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "cis")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_4a_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_4a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_4a, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_4a, name = names(comp_IVWs_4a), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from GWAS common and WES ultra-rare cis-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_4b <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_estimates, 
                       IVset1 = "sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped",
                       IVset2 = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3,
                       cis_trans = "trans")

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_4b_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_4b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_4b, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_4b, name = names(comp_IVWs_4b), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from GWAS common and WES ultra-rare trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

# Extract list of genes with significant heterogenity between cis common and rare/ultrarare variant estimates
het_genes <- lapply(
  Map(c, lapply(comp_IVWs_1a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_2a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_3a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_4a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)})), function(x){unique(x)})

het_genes <- mapply(FUN = function(x,y){paste(x,y,sep = "_")}, x = het_genes, y = names(het_genes), SIMPLIFY = F)
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-compIVs-GWAScommonvrare-concordance
#| fig.width: 7
#| fig.height: 5

plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_3a_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: GWAS common vs rare cis-IVs")
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_3b_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: GWAS common vs rare trans-IVs") 

plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_4a_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: GWAS common vs ultrarare cis-IVs")
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_4b_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: GWAS common vs ultrarare trans-IVs") 

```

**Overlap between protein:outcome pairs with significant causal effectes estimated across cis-IV sets**

```{r}
#| label: upset-compIVs-common
#| fig.width: 8
#| fig.height: 3
#| eval: FALSE

plot_upset(IVsets_list = c("sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped","dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare","dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare"), cis_trans = "cis", pval = 0.05)

```

7)  Common cis-IVs vs trans-IVs (GWAS)\
8)  Common cis-IVs vs trans-IVs (ExWAS)\
9)  Rare cis-IVs vs trans-IVs (ExWAS)\
10) Ultra-rare cis-IVs vs trans-IVs\

```{r}
#| label: fig-compIVs-cisvtrans
#| cache: TRUE
#| message: FALSE
#| fig.width: 14
#| fig.height: 6

comp_IVWs_5 <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_cistrans, 
                      IVset = "sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped",
                      n_snps = 3)

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_5_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_5, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_5, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_5, name = names(comp_IVWs_5), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from GWAS common and cis- and trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_6 <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_cistrans, 
                      IVset = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_common",
                      n_snps = 3)

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_6_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_6, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_6, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_6, name = names(comp_IVWs_6), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from ExWAS common and cis- and trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_7 <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_cistrans, 
                      IVset = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned",
                      n_snps = 3)

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_7_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_7, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_7, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_7, name = names(comp_IVWs_7), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from ExWAS rare and cis- and trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

comp_IVWs_8 <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, FUN = compare_cistrans, 
                       IVset = "dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned",
                       n_snps = 3)

# Proportion concordant
comp_IVWs_8_conc <- list("all" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_8, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$all})),
                          "Qsig" = do.call("rbind", lapply(lapply(comp_IVWs_8, FUN = prop_concordant), function(x){x$sigQ})))

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = mapply(plot_estimates, estimates = comp_IVWs_8, name = names(comp_IVWs_8), SIMPLIFY = FALSE), nrow = 2,
                        top = "Comparison of IVW estimates from ExWAS ultra-rare and cis- and trans-pQTL instrumentation strategies")

# Extract list of genes with significant heterogenity between cis and trans estimates
het_genes_cistrans <- lapply(
  Map(c, lapply(comp_IVWs_5, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_6, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_7, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_8, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval <= 0.01) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)})), function(x){unique(x)})

het_genes_cistrans <- mapply(FUN = function(x,y){paste(x,y,sep = "_")}, x = het_genes_cistrans , y = names(het_genes_cistrans), SIMPLIFY = F)

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-compIVs-cisvtans-concordance
#| fig.width: 9
#| fig.height: 5

plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_5_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: GWAS common cis vs trans-IVs")
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_6_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES common cis vs trans-IVs") 
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_7_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES rare cis vs trans-IVs")
plot_concordance(concordance = comp_IVWs_8_conc, title = "IVW estimate concordance: WES common ultrarare cis vs trans-IVs") 
```

### Heterogenity vs pleiotropy profile

If the cis-pleiotropy profile of common and rare IVs for a gene is more consistant (greater overlap in the genes instrumented), are the MR effect estimates more likely to be consistent?

```{r}
#|label: heterogenity-vs-pleiotropy

# Pull list of all cis pqtls used as instruments in the relaxed threshold analysis
# exome::rare and genome::common for this analysis

varIDs_relaxed <- list("dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned" = 
                         do.call("rbind", lapply(harmonised_snps_relaxed$BMI, function(x){x["dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned"][[1]]})) |> 
                         dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis", mr_keep == TRUE) |> pull(SNP) |> unique(),
                       "sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped" = 
                         do.call("rbind", lapply(harmonised_snps_relaxed$BMI, function(x){
                           dat <- x["sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped"][[1]]
                           if(all(is.na(dat$SNP))){return(NULL)}
                           dat[,c("variant","cis_trans","mr_keep")]})) |> 
                         dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis", mr_keep == TRUE) |> pull(variant) |> unique())
# Correct SNP ID formatting
varIDs_relaxed <- lapply(varIDs_relaxed, function(x){toupper(gsub("_","-",sub(":","-",x)))})


## HERE 


# Calculate proportion of cis and trans associated genes that are shared between common and rare IVs for a given exposure
# As done above, but using all variants, not just the most pleiotropic











# Summarise associations per variant (pQTL)
pQTLs_assocs_MRset <- mapply(function(x, y){
  x <- x |> dplyr::group_by(variant, proximal_gene) |> 
    dplyr::summarise(n_assocs = n(), 
                     n_cis = length(which(cis_trans == "cis")),
                     n_truecis = sum(proximal_gene == paste0("'",protein_target,"'")),
                     n_trans = length(which(cis_trans == "trans")),
                     assoc_genes = paste(unique(protein_target), collapse = ";"),
                     assoc_cis_genes = paste(unique(protein_target[cis_trans == "cis"]), collapse = ";"),
                     assoc_trans_genes = paste(unique(protein_target[cis_trans == "trans"]), collapse = ";"))
  x$IV_set <- y
  return(x)
}, x = lookup_pQTLs_unpruned, y = as.list(names(lookup_pQTLs_unpruned)), SIMPLIFY = F)

pqtls_assocs_joined_MRset <- do.call("rbind", pQTLs_assocs_MRset) |>
  dplyr::mutate(IV_set = factor(IVsets[IV_set], levels = IVsets))










# Pull list of all cis pqtls used as instruments in the relaxed threshold analysis
# exome::rare and genome::common for this analysis
pqtls_MRset <- pull_pqtls(snpset = harmonised_snps_relaxed, IVsetnames = c("dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned","sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped"))
names(pqtls_MRset) <- names_genes

pqtls_MRset <- sapply(c("dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned","sun_gwas_variant_common_clumped"), 
                      function(x){do.call(rbind, lapply(pqtls_MRset, `[[`, x))} |> 
                        na.exclude() |> 
                        dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis") |>
                        dplyr::pull(SNP) |>
                        unique(), simplify = F)

# Extract variants used for MR
pQTLs_assocs_MRset$dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare <- pQTLs_assocs_MRset$dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare$variant %in% toupper(pqtls_MRset)


```



### Heterogenity in causal effects (examples)

Restricting to those protein exposures that show signficiant causal effects on a given outcome (at p \<= 0.05 with \>= 3 IVs), and significant heterogenity between rare and common cis variant IV sets (at Qstat P(FDR) \< 0.05):

```{r}
#| label: heterogenous effects
#| cache: TRUE

# Retain exposure-outcome pairs with significant IVW estimate for at least one cis instrumentation strategy (with n instruments >= n_snps)
res_MR_top <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, filter_results, p_thresh = 0.05, n_snps = 3, cistrans = "cis")
res_MR_top_cistrans <- lapply(res_MR_relaxed, filter_results, p_thresh = 0.05, n_snps = 3, cistrans = c("cis","trans"))

# Filter to those with evidence of estimate heterogenity between variant sets
res_MR_top_het <- mapply(function(x,y){x[names(x) %in% y]}, x = res_MR_top, y = het_genes, SIMPLIFY = F)

# Filter to those with evidence of estimate heterogeneity between cis and trans IVs
res_MR_top_cistrans_het <- mapply(function(x,y){x[names(x) %in% y]}, x = res_MR_top, y = het_genes_cistrans, SIMPLIFY = F)

```

**Heterogeneity in rare v common cis IV causal estimates**

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots1
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 3.5
#| results: 'hide'

# Plot estimates
res_IVW_cis <- lapply(res_MR_top_het, function(x){
  df <- do.call("rbind", x) |> 
    filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted",
           grepl("variant",IV_set),
           cis_trans == "cis") |>
    dplyr::mutate(instrument_class = factor(variant_IVsets[IV_set], levels = rev(variant_IVsets)))
  return(df)})

plot_forest <- function(results_IVW){
  pd <- ggplot2::position_dodge2(width = 0.8, preserve = "single")
  exposure <- unique(sub(".*_", "", results_IVW$pair))
  
  n_row <- ceiling(length(unique(results_IVW$exposure))/4)
  
  p <- ggplot(results_IVW, aes(x = b, y = instrument_class, colour = instrument_class)) +
    facet_wrap(.~pair, ncol = 5, scale = "free_x") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point(aes(size = nsnp), position = pd) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = b-1.96*se, xmax = b+1.96*se, group = cis_trans), position = pd, height = 0.4) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    theme_bw() + guides(colour = "none") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5), axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7)) +
    labs(x = "IVW", y = "", size = "n (instruments)", title = paste("Outcome:", exposure))
  
  return(p)
}

lapply(res_IVW_cis, plot_forest)[c(1,4,6,7,8,9,10)]
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots2
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 2
#| results: 'hide'

lapply(res_IVW_cis, plot_forest)[c(3,5,11)]

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots3
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 5
#| results: 'hide'

lapply(res_IVW_cis, plot_forest)[c(2)]

```

**Heterogeneity in cis v trans IV causal estimates**

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots-cistrans-1
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 4
#| results: 'hide'

# Plot estimates
res_IVW_cistrans <- lapply(res_MR_top_cistrans_het, function(x){
  df <- do.call("rbind", x) |> 
    filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted",
           grepl("variant",IV_set),
           cis_trans %in% c("cis","trans")) |>
    dplyr::mutate(instrument_class = factor(variant_IVsets[IV_set], levels = rev(variant_IVsets)))
  return(df)})

plot_forest_cistrans <- function(results_IVW){
  pd <- ggplot2::position_dodge2(width = 0.8, preserve = "single")
  exposure <- unique(sub(".*_", "", results_IVW$pair))
  
  n_row <- ceiling(length(unique(results_IVW$exposure))/4)
  
  p <- ggplot(results_IVW, aes(x = b, y = instrument_class, colour = instrument_class)) +
    facet_wrap(.~pair, ncol = 5, scale = "free_x") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point(aes(pch = cis_trans, size = nsnp), position = pd) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = b-1.96*se, xmax = b+1.96*se, group = cis_trans), position = pd) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    scale_shape_manual(name = "", values = c(16,1)) +
    theme_bw() + guides(colour = "none") +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) +
    labs(x = "IVW", y = "", size = "n (instruments)", title = paste("Outcome:", exposure))
  
  return(p)
}

lapply(res_IVW_cistrans, plot_forest_cistrans)[c(1,2,3,4,7,9)]
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots-cistrans-2
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 2.5
#| results: 'hide'

lapply(res_IVW_cistrans, plot_forest_cistrans)[c(6,8,10,11)]

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots-cistrans-3
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 5.5
#| results: 'hide'

lapply(res_IVW_cistrans, plot_forest_cistrans)[c(5)]

```

**Concordance vs discordance in cis and trans effects**

How often do causal estimates for a protein:outcome effect derived from cis- and trans-pQTLs agree (in terms of significance and direction of effect)? 

### Consistency in causal effects (examples)

Restricting to those protein exposures that show signficiant causal effects on a given outcome (at p \<= 0.05 with \>= 3 IVs) supported by rare and common cis variant IV sets (Qsat P(FDR) \< 0.05):

```{r}
#| label: consistent effects
#| fig.width: 10.5
#| fig.height: 5

# Extract list of genes with consistent (significant) effects between cis common and rare/ultrarare variant estimates
consistant_genes <- lapply(
  Map(c, 
      lapply(comp_IVWs_1a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval > 0.1 & x[,6] < 0.05 & x[,7] < 0.05 & sign(x[,2]) == sign(x[,3])) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_2a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval > 0.1 & x[,6] < 0.05 & x[,7] < 0.05 & sign(x[,2]) == sign(x[,3])) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_3a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval > 0.1 & x[,6] < 0.05 & x[,7] < 0.05 & sign(x[,2]) == sign(x[,3])) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)}),
      lapply(comp_IVWs_4a, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(Qpval > 0.1 & x[,6] < 0.05 & x[,7] < 0.05 & sign(x[,2]) == sign(x[,3])) |> dplyr::pull(exposure)})), function(x){unique(x)})

consistant_genes <- mapply(FUN = function(x,y){paste(x,y,sep = "_")}, x = consistant_genes, y = names(consistant_genes), SIMPLIFY = F)

# Filter to significant MR results supported across IV sets
res_MR_top_consistant <- mapply(function(x,y){x[names(x) %in% y]}, x = res_MR_top, y = consistant_genes, SIMPLIFY = F)

res_IVW_consistant <- lapply(res_MR_top_consistant, function(x){
  df <- do.call("rbind", x) |> 
    filter(method == "Inverse variance weighted",
           grepl("variant",IV_set),
           cis_trans == "cis") |>
    dplyr::mutate(instrument_class = factor(variant_IVsets[IV_set], levels = rev(variant_IVsets)))
  return(df)})
```

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots-consistant-1
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 2
#| results: 'hide'

lapply(res_IVW_consistant, plot_forest)[c(1,2,5,6,7,8,9,10,11)]

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-forestplots-consistant-2
#| fig.width: 8.5
#| fig.height: 3.5
#| results: 'hide'

lapply(res_IVW_consistant, plot_forest)[c(3,4)]

```

## Case study: SMPD1-SBP
```{r}
#| label: case-study
#| fig.width: 6
#| fig.height: 4

## MR with LD pruned rare variants (in cis region, including outside of gene):
plot_results(exposure = "SMPD1", outcome = "SBP")
```

```{r}
#| label: case-study-wrs
#| fig.width: 7
#| fig.height: 6


### FUNCTION NOW ABOVE



## Filter to cis variants in the gene itself and repeat MR
ivs_SMPD1 <- lapply(harmonised_snps_relaxed$SBP$SMPD1_SBP, function(x){x |> dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis")})
ivs_SMPD1$dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned_truecis <- ivs_SMPD1$dhindsa_exwas_variant_ultrarare_pruned |> 
  dplyr::filter(gene.outcome == "SMPD1")
ivs_SMPD1$dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned_truecis <- ivs_SMPD1$dhindsa_exwas_variant_rare_pruned |> 
  dplyr::filter(gene.outcome == "SMPD1")

mr_SMPD1 <- lapply(ivs_SMPD1, function(x){TwoSampleMR::mr(x)})

## Derive wald ratios for all SMPD1 cis-IVs, all frequencies
keep_cols <- c("SNP","variant","chr.exposure","pos.exposure","beta.exposure","beta.outcome",
               "se.exposure","se.outcome","pval.exposure","pval.outcome",
               "eaf.exposure","cis_trans","gene.outcome", "ANNOTATON")

ivs_SMPD1_all <- do.call("bind_rows", mapply(FUN = function(x,y){
  keep_cols_df <- intersect(keep_cols, colnames(x))
  x <- x[,keep_cols_df]
  x$IV_set <- y
  return(x)}, x = ivs_SMPD1[names(variant_IVsets)], y = variant_IVsets, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)) |>
  dplyr::mutate(chr.exposure = ifelse(grepl("rs", SNP), sub(":.*","",variant), chr.exposure),
                pos.exposure = ifelse(grepl("rs", SNP), sub(".*:(.*)_.*_.*","\\1",variant), pos.exposure),
                SNP = toupper(ifelse(grepl("rs", SNP), variant, SNP))) |>
  dplyr::arrange(pos.exposure) |>
  dplyr::filter(duplicated(SNP) == FALSE)

wrs_SMPD1_all <- t(apply(ivs_SMPD1_all, MARGIN = 1, function(x){
  TwoSampleMR::mr_wald_ratio(b_exp = as.numeric(x["beta.exposure"]),
                             b_out = as.numeric(x["beta.outcome"]), 
                             se_exp = as.numeric(x["se.exposure"]),
                             se_out = as.numeric(x["se.outcome"])) |> unlist()
}))
colnames(wrs_SMPD1_all) <- paste0("wald_ratio_", colnames(wrs_SMPD1_all))
wrs_SMPD1_all <- cbind(ivs_SMPD1_all, wrs_SMPD1_all)

# Full SNP information including aa coding changes:
deets_SMPD1 <- data.table::fread(file.path(data_dir,"sumstats/proteomics/SMPD1snps_allchr11.txt")) |>
  dplyr::mutate(SNP_join = gsub("-","_",sub("-",":",Variant)))

wrs_SMPD1_all <- left_join(wrs_SMPD1_all, 
                           deets_SMPD1[,c("SNP_join","cDNA change","Amino acid change","Exon rank","ANNOTATION")],
                           by = c("SNP" = "SNP_join"))


# ivs = harmonised SNP data with appendend wald ratios and variant details
plot_forest_snps <- function(iv_effects){
  iv_effects$SNP_name = paste0("chr",sub("_.*","",iv_effects$SNP),"\n",iv_effects$`Amino acid change`)
  
  f1 <- ggplot(iv_effects, aes(x = SNP_name, y = beta.exposure, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = beta.exposure - 1.96*se.exposure,
                      ymax = beta.exposure + 1.96*se.exposure), width = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "", y = "Bx")
  
  f2 <- ggplot(iv_effects, aes(x = SNP_name, y = beta.outcome, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = beta.outcome - 1.96*se.outcome,
                      ymax = beta.outcome + 1.96*se.outcome), width = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_blank(), legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "", y = "By")
  
  f3 <- ggplot(iv_effects, aes(x = SNP_name, y = wald_ratio_b, colour = IV_set)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    geom_point(size = 2.5) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = wald_ratio_b - 1.96*wald_ratio_se,
                      ymax = wald_ratio_b + 1.96*wald_ratio_se), width = 0.3) +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_IVsets) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
          legend.position = "none") +
    labs(x = "", y = "By/Bx")
  
  gridExtra::grid.arrange(f1, f2, f3, heights = c(0.26,0.26,0.48))
}

plot_forest_snps(iv_effects = wrs_SMPD1_all)

```

```{r}
#| label: SMPD1-geneview
#| fig.width: 18
#| fig.height: 3

genome_axis <- GenomeAxisTrack()
# MANE-select transcripts
mane_ensembl <- rtracklayer::import(file.path(data_dir, "MANE.GRCh38.v1.5.ensembl_genomic.gtf.gz"))

plot_genomefeatures <- function(chr, regionstart, regionstop, marker_positions){
  
  region <- GRanges(seqnames = chr, ranges = IRanges(regionstart, regionstop))
  # Extract reference annotations in region
  mane_region <- mane_ensembl[mane_ensembl %over% region]
  mane_region <- mane_region[mcols(mane_region)$type == "exon" & mcols(mane_region)$tag == "MANE_Select"]
  
  # Genome ranges
  grtrack <- GeneRegionTrack(
    mane_region,
    chromosome = chr,
    name = "Gene Model",
    transcript = "transcript_id",
    exon = "exon_id",
    gene = "gene_id",
    symbol = "gene_name",
    stacking = "full",
    collapseTranscripts = FALSE,
    showId = FALSE
  )
  
  displayPars(grtrack) <- list(
    transcriptAnnotation = "gene_name",
    just.group = "above")
  
  # Convert to snp positons to GRanges object
  snp_gr <- GRanges(
    seqnames = marker_positions$chr,
    ranges = IRanges(start = marker_positions$pos, width = 1))
  
  # SNP annotation track
  snptrack <- AnnotationTrack(
    snp_gr,
    name = "SNPs",
    shape = "box",
    stacking = "dense")
  
  plotTracks(list(genome_axis, grtrack, snptrack), from = regionstart, to = regionstop)
}

iv_positions = data.frame(chr = "chr11",
                          pos = as.numeric(wrs_SMPD1_all$pos.exposure))

# Zoom in
plot_genomefeatures(chr = "chr11", regionstart = 6390000, regionstop = 6400000, marker_positions = iv_positions)
# Zoom out
plot_genomefeatures(chr = "chr11", regionstart = 6240000, regionstop = 6601000, marker_positions = iv_positions)

```

## Allelic series

Assessing for monotonically increasing causal effect estimates (Wald Ratios) across rare-variant series of alleles:\
- Rare variants (\<1% MAF): benign missense variants (BMV) - damaging missense variants (DMVs) - protein truncating variants (PTVs)\

Using the [COAST-SS](https://github.com/insitro/AllelicSeries) approach (developed for allelic series association tests), which has been developed to handle GWAS summary statistics, but here we are passing Wald Ratios (variant outcome effect/ variant protein effect)

What is needed here?: \
- Summary statistics for all rare variants (capped at p<0.01 by the AzPheWAS portal for pQTLs) falling in the coding region of genes encoding the O'link plasma proteome assayed proteins\
- The corresponding variant associations for outcomes of interest (with which to calculate Wald ratios)\ 
- Variant predicted functional effects (with which to categorise SNPs)

**Magnitude of variant class effects**

Do cis-variants predicted to be more deleterious in nature (loss of function) actually have larger effects on protein expression?

```{r}
#| label: vep-classes
#| fig.width: 7
#| fig.height: 3

# Read in variant effect estimates, wald ratios and VEP annotations
load(file.path(res_dir,"results_molecular_waldratios_ciscodingvariants_VEPannotated.rda"))

wald_ratios <- lapply(wald_ratios, function(x){
  x$annots_relaxed <- as.factor(x$annots_relaxed)
  x})

vep_class <- c("1" = "syn./noncoding", 
               "2" = "benign missense",
               "3" = "del. missense",
               "4" = "putative LoF")

cols_vep <- c("1" = "steelblue", 
              "2" = "olivedrab3",
              "3" = "orange",
              "4" = "tomato")

# Plot variant consequnce call on cis pQTL effect
wald_ratios$LDL |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(annots_relaxed)) |> 
  ggplot(aes(x = beta.exposure, 
             y = annots_relaxed)) +
  ggridges::geom_density_ridges(aes(fill = annots_relaxed), trim = TRUE) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour ="grey30", linewidth = 1) + 
  scale_y_discrete(labels = vep_class) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_vep) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  labs(x = "cis-pQTL effect (beta)", y = "Variant annotation")

# Plot wald ratios split by variant consequence
plot_wald_vep <- function(gene, outcome){
  dat <- wald_ratios[[outcome]] |> 
    dplyr::filter(exposure == gene) |>
    dplyr::arrange(annots_relaxed, wald_ratio_b) |>
    dplyr::mutate(SNP = factor(SNP, levels = SNP))
  
  res <- coastss_annots_relaxed[[outcome]] |>
    dplyr::filter(exposure == gene)
  
  p_theme <- theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
          legend.position = "bottom",
          legend.key.height = unit(0.2, 'cm'))
  
  # Exposure
  p_exp <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = beta.exposure, y = SNP, colour = annots_relaxed)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point() +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_vep, labels = vep_class, name = "") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = beta.exposure-1.96*se.exposure, xmax = beta.exposure+1.96*se.exposure)) +
    labs(x = "Bx", y = "", 
         title = paste("Exposure:", gene),
         subtitle = "") +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=2, byrow=TRUE)) +
    p_theme
  
  # Outcome
  p_out <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = beta.outcome, y = SNP, colour = annots_relaxed)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point() +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_vep, labels = vep_class, name = "") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = beta.outcome-1.96*se.outcome, xmax = beta.outcome+1.96*se.outcome)) +
    labs(x = "By", y = "", 
         title = paste("Outcome:", outcome),
         subtitle = "") +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=2, byrow=TRUE)) +
    p_theme
  
  # Wald Ratio
  p_wald <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = wald_ratio_b, y = SNP, colour = annots_relaxed)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point() +
    scale_colour_manual(values = cols_vep, labels = vep_class, name = "") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = wald_ratio_b-1.96*wald_ratio_se, xmax = wald_ratio_b+1.96*wald_ratio_se)) +
    labs(x = "Wald Ratio (By/Bx)", y = "", 
         title = paste("Exposure:", gene, "Outcome:", outcome),
         subtitle = paste("COAST-SS p-value:", signif(res$alleleic_skat_p, 2))) +
    guides(colour = guide_legend(nrow=2, byrow=TRUE)) +
    p_theme
  
  return(gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = list(p_wald, p_exp, p_out), nrow = 1))
}

```

### COAST-SS analysis

```{r}
#| label: COAST-SS 
#| fig.width: 12
#| fig.height: 20

# Load results from COAST-SS allelic series anlysis 
# 1 = synonymous/noncoding, 2 = benign missense, 3 = deleterious missense, 4 = putative loss of funcion

load(file.path(res_dir,"results_molecular_COASTSS_relaxed.rda")) # coastss_annots_relaxed

## Filter out exposure genes for which there were insufficient variants for alleleic series analysis and calculate adjusted p-vals
coastss_annots_relaxed <- lapply(coastss_annots_relaxed, function(x){
  x |> 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(alleleic_skat_p)) |>
    dplyr::mutate(adj_p = p.adjust(alleleic_skat_p, method = "BH"))
})

outcome_names <- c("LDL direct" = "LDL",
                    "Body mass index (BMI)" = "BMI",
                    "Vitamin D" = "Vitamin D",
                    "Triglycerides" = "Triglycerides",
                    "Glycated haemoglobin (HbA1c)" = "HbA1c",
                    "Mean platelet (thrombocyte) volume" = "PlatVol",
                    "IGF-1" = "IGF-1",
                    "Waist-to-hip ratio adjusted for body mass index (WHRadjBMI)" = "WHRadjBMI",
                    "Red blood cell (erythrocyte) count" = "RBCcount",
                    "Mean corpuscular volume" = "CorpVol",
                    "Systolic blood pressure, automated reading" = "SBP")
                    

do.call("rbind", coastss_annots_relaxed) |>
  dplyr::mutate(label = ifelse(adj_p < 0.05, exposure, NA),
                outcome = outcome_names[outcome]) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = exposure, y = -log10(alleleic_skat_p))) +
  facet_grid(outcome ~ ., scale = "free_y") +
  geom_point(aes(colour = adj_p < 0.05)) +
  scale_colour_manual(name = "P(FDR) < 0.05", values = c("grey","tomato")) +
  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(aes(label = label), size = 4, max.overlaps = Inf, force = 10) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 10),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 14),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  labs(x = "Protein exposure", y = "-log10(P) Allelic series")
```

**Dopamine Beta Hydrozylase (DBH) on systolic blood pressure**

```{r}
#| label: DBH-SBP 
#| fig.width: 12
#| fig.height: 4

plot_wald_vep(gene = "DBH", outcome = "SBP")
```

## Gene based analysis

In gene based analysis, variant aggregate approaches are used to test for the effect of carrying any single or combination of rare genetic variants in a gene on a phenotype. Variant masks are often generated based on variant properties (e.g putative loss of function or missense variants), allowing the consequence of varying degrees of gene perturbation to be assessed. Here gene-based masks have been testing for cis and trans associations with plasma protein levels and complex traits, allowing them to be used in an MR framework. It is perhaps most straight forward to handle gene-level associations as single instuments and interpret Wald ratios, although in some case multiple gene-level instruments can exist for a given exposure.

```{r}
#| label: extract-masks

# Extract gene-based burden mask and calculate Wald ratios
res_masks <- lapply(harmonised_snps, function(x){
  masks <- lapply(x, function(y){
    dat <- y[IV_sets[1:4]] # gene mask results
    # Remove empty results dfs
    dat <- dat[unlist(lapply(dat, function(x){all(!(is.na(x$SNP)))}))]
    
    dat <- do.call("rbind", lapply(dat, function(z){
      z |> dplyr::select(
        mask = test.exposure, 
        mask_gene = SNP, 
        chr = chr.outcome, 
        pos = pos.outcome, 
        exposure, outcome, 
        beta.exposure, beta.outcome, 
        se.exposure, se.outcome, 
        pval.exposure, pval.outcome, 
        cis_trans)
    }))
  })
})

res_masks <- lapply(res_masks, function(x){
  out <- do.call("rbind",x)
  rownames(out) <- NULL
  return(out)})

# Wald ratios
res_masks <- lapply(res_masks, function(x){
  wrs <- apply(x, MARGIN = 1, FUN = function(x){
    TwoSampleMR::mr_wald_ratio(
    b_exp = as.numeric(x["beta.exposure"]),
    b_out = as.numeric(x["beta.outcome"]),
    se_exp = as.numeric(x["se.exposure"]),
    se_out = as.numeric(x["se.outcome"]))
  })

  wrs_df <- do.call("rbind",lapply(wrs, unlist))
  colnames(wrs_df) <- paste("wald_ratio", colnames(wrs_df), sep = "_")

  # Bind to harmonised data
  out <- cbind(x,wrs_df)
  return(out)
})
```

**Gene-level cis-mask associations**

Focusing on aggregate tests combining putative loss of function (protein truncating) and damaging rare varaints, which genes show significant causal effects on outcomes of interest:

```{r}
#| label: fig-maskcis_IVs
#| fig.width: 22
#| fig.height: 8
  
res_masks_cis <- lapply(res_masks, function(x){
  out <- x |> 
    dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "cis",
                            mask == "ptvraredmg") |> 
    dplyr::mutate(wald_ratio_pval_adj = p.adjust(wald_ratio_pval, method = "BH")) |>
    dplyr::arrange(wald_ratio_pval_adj)
  return(out)
})

# Plot Wald ratios for all gene maks associated with an outcome
plot_wald_masks <- function(results_masks, outcome, pthresh){
  dat <- results_masks[[outcome]] |> 
    dplyr::filter(wald_ratio_pval_adj < pthresh) |>
    dplyr::arrange(wald_ratio_b) |>
    dplyr::mutate(
      mask_gene = toupper(mask_gene),
      mask_gene = factor(mask_gene, levels = mask_gene))

  p_wald <- ggplot(dat, aes(x = wald_ratio_b, y = mask_gene)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, colour = "grey20", lty = 2) +
    geom_point(colour = "steelblue4", size = 2) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = wald_ratio_b-1.96*wald_ratio_se, xmax = wald_ratio_b+1.96*wald_ratio_se),
                   colour = "steelblue4",
                   height = 0.4) +
    labs(x = "Wald Ratio (By/Bx)", y = "Burden test gene \n (instrumenting cis-protein expression)", 
         title = paste("Outcome:", outcome),
         subtitle = "cis-IVs (pLOF and rare damaging)") +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
  
  return(p_wald)}

gridExtra::grid.arrange(grobs = lapply(names(res_masks_cis)[-1], plot_wald_masks, results_masks = res_masks_cis, pthresh = 0.1),
                        nrow = 2)
```

**Cis vs trans gene-level mask associations**

```{r}

res_masks_trans <- lapply(res_masks, function(x){
  out <- x |> 
    dplyr::filter(cis_trans == "trans",
```
